name: Urlbox
what_it_does: Captures pixel-perfect screenshots, PDFs, and metadata of any URL using a managed headless browser fleet.
use_cases: Generate website screenshots for visual regression testing in CI/CD pipelines
Capture full-page PDF snapshots of legal or compliance web pages for archival agents
Monitor competitor landing pages by taking scheduled screenshots for change detection
Produce OG image previews of URLs for link preview features in applications
Render and capture JavaScript-heavy SPAs that require full browser execution
not_for: Extracting structured data from web pages (use a dedicated scraping API instead)
Automating multi-step user interactions or form submissions on websites
High-frequency real-time monitoring at sub-second intervals
best_when: You need a reliable, managed screenshot service with fine-grained rendering controls without managing your own browser infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need interactive browser automation or structured data extraction rather than visual capture.

auth.notes: API key used directly; HMAC token signing available for secure URL-based access to prevent key exposure in client-side code.

pricing.paid_tiers: Plans from ~$29/month for 1,000 renders; scales to enterprise with custom pricing
pricing.notes: Renders include screenshots, PDFs, and metadata calls; async rendering available on higher plans.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent request limits apply per plan; async mode recommended for batch workloads

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors are returned with HTTP codes and descriptive messages; timeout errors may not clearly indicate root cause.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Screenshot captures are read-only and stateless; same URL and parameters produce consistent results.
known_agent_gotchas: Pages with bot detection (Cloudflare, reCAPTCHA) may return blank or error screenshots without a clear failure signal
The wait_for parameter must be tuned per target site; incorrect values cause premature or excessively delayed captures
HMAC token expiry must be managed by the agent; expired tokens return 401 without explicit expiry information in the error
Full-page screenshots of very long pages can produce extremely large image files that exceed downstream size limits
Cookie and header injection for authenticated pages requires careful session management; cookies may expire between agent calls

security_score_components.security_notes: HMAC signing prevents key exposure in URLs; API key must still be protected server-side; SSRF considerations apply when accepting user-supplied URLs.
